steps.title: Identify the right triangle and apply Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
The right angle is at $S$, making $TR$ the hypotenuse. We have $TR = 79$ and $RS = \sqrt{157}$. Use the Pythagorean theorem to find the remaining leg $TS$:

$$TR^2 = TS^2 + RS^2$$

steps.title: Solve for the opposite side TS
Calculate sin R using the right triangle definition
steps.content:
From angle $R$, the opposite side is $TS$ and the hypotenuse is $TR$:

$$\sin R = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{TS}{TR} = \frac{78}{79}$$
